title: Augustinian Redemptive History
author: Augustine (drawn from his theological corpus)
source: Augustine of Hippo's four states of human will (posse peccare / non posse peccare etc.) and the Creation–Fall–Salvation–Eternity arc — the spine of the Christian metanarrative.

lineage.note: The Bible's macro-structure read as a four-act story. The deepest single reason redemption arcs feel archetypal in Western storytelling.

stages.name: Fall
stages.function: Rupture; sin enters; original order broken.

stages.name: Salvation
stages.function: Redemption offered; broken order under repair.

stages.name: Eternity
stages.function: Restored order; the new heaven and new earth.

applicability.limitations: Theological framework; using it editorially requires being aware of its baggage.
